# ellmos Blender Use MCP

> Local-first, add-on-free MCP asset-QA tool for headless Blender FBX reimport verification and bounded background script runs.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/ellmos-blender-use-mcp
npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/ellmos-blender-use-mcp
MCP registry name: io.github.ellmos-ai/ellmos-blender-use-mcp
License: MIT
Language: JavaScript (ESM)
Runtime: Node.js >=18

## Summary

ellmos Blender Use MCP is an alpha-stage Model Context Protocol server built for game and 3D asset pipelines. Its core capability is `blender_verify_fbx_reimport`: it imports a given FBX into headless Blender and writes a deterministic JSON verification result with mesh count, material count, and any missing required naming prefixes, replacing a manual reimport-and-eyeball check. `blender_run_script` and `blender_locate` are the general-purpose primitives the verification tool is built on: running an arbitrary Python script in `blender --background` mode with a bounded, timeout-guarded output tail, and resolving the local Blender executable.

It does not install a Blender add-on, does not open a TCP port for a running Blender instance, and does not keep Blender resident between calls — every call is a fresh, headless, stateless `blender --background` process with a bounded timeout. It does not download assets and does not collect telemetry.

It is useful for CI-style, one-shot asset verification in an asset pipeline. It is not a live-GUI Blender controller; for interactive scene editing over a persistent connection, use a dedicated add-on-based Blender MCP server instead.

## Tools

- `blender_verify_fbx_reimport`: import an FBX in headless Blender and write/read a JSON verification result (mesh count, material count, missing required prefixes).
- `blender_run_script`: run `blender --background --python <script.py>` with optional script arguments and a bounded stdout/stderr tail.
- `blender_locate`: resolve the local Blender executable from an explicit path, `BLENDER_EXE`, a verified local default, or `PATH`.

## Install

```bash
npx -y ellmos-blender-use-mcp
```

or install from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ellmos-ai/ellmos-blender-use-mcp.git
cd ellmos-blender-use-mcp
npm install
node src/index.js
```

## Audience

- Technical artists and asset pipeline engineers who want an automated, deterministic pass/fail check for FBX reimport instead of manually reopening files in Blender
- Game studios and 3D asset teams running CI checks on exported assets (mesh/material counts, required naming prefixes)
- Developers who need a headless, add-on-free, no-open-port way to run bounded Blender Python scripts from an MCP client
- AI assistants (Claude, Codex, Gemini) automating Blender-based asset QA steps in a build or delivery pipeline

## Search Phrases

```
blender fbx reimport verification mcp
headless blender asset qa mcp server
blender mcp no add-on no tcp port
blender background script mcp server
mcp server fbx mesh material verification
asset pipeline blender automation mcp
ellmos blender use mcp
ellmos-blender-use-mcp
```

## External Discovery Notes

- Several other "Blender MCP" projects exist (e.g. `ahujasid/blender-mcp`, the official Blender Labs MCP server) and dominate generic "blender mcp" searches; they drive a live, running Blender GUI over a TCP/add-on bridge, which is a different use case and trust model than this headless asset-QA tool. Use the full package name or "fbx reimport verification" / "asset QA" phrasing to disambiguate.
- npm and GitHub exact-repo/package-name discovery work for `ellmos-blender-use-mcp`.
- Good listing context: headless Blender automation, FBX/asset verification, game-asset CI pipelines, add-on-free MCP servers.

## Important Files

- `README.md`: full English documentation.
- `README_de.md`: German documentation.
- `server.json`: MCP registry metadata.
- `THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md`: dependency and prior-art license review.
- `src/index.js`: MCP server entrypoint and tool definitions.
- `test/privacy-hygiene.test.js`: regression test guarding against leaked local user-profile paths.

## Last-checked: 2026-07-06
